product_summary: OutFlow is a workspace-scoped SaaS workflow system for outbound agencies running personalized B2B cold email. It combines campaign context, prospect research, reviewed draft generation, reply handling, and Gmail draft handoff in one product, with subscription-gated access.

confirmed: Outbound agencies running B2B client campaigns
inferred_from_repo: Small-to-mid agency teams needing controlled personalization workflows

current_features.feature: Subscription gating and Stripe checkout/portal/webhook sync
Research, sequence generation, reply analysis and drafting
Schema-based contracts and validation-first AI outputs
Gmail OAuth connection, thread import, draft handoff
Provider-agnostic inbox model including Microsoft365 values
Workspace export and internal dataset export pipeline

workflow_steps: User lands on public site and chooses create-account or sign-in.
User authenticates through Supabase-backed routes and callback.
Subscription gate allows app usage only for active plan workspaces.
User configures sender profile and campaign context.
User adds prospects and runs research snapshots.
User generates sequences and reviews/edits/regenerates drafts.
User handles inbound replies with analysis and draft suggestions.
User can connect Gmail to import threads/messages and push drafts.
System records usage/audit/signal data and supports export routes.

architecture_summary.frontend: Next.js App Router with separate public and authenticated route surfaces.
architecture_summary.backend_api: Server actions and route handlers under apps/web/app/api with server modules in apps/web/lib/server.
architecture_summary.database_storage: Postgres schema and migrations in packages/database with workspace-scoped entities and inbox tables.
architecture_summary.ai_model_calls: Provider abstraction with OpenAI and Anthropic adapters; validation contracts enforced by engine packages.
architecture_summary.job_processing: Async-ready states and jobs package foundation; not a fully externalized queue runtime by default.
architecture_summary.security_boundaries: Workspace-scoped app checks plus RLS and role policies in SQL migrations.

gaps_and_limitations: Inbox provider parity is incomplete (Gmail concrete path exists; Microsoft365 appears schema/contract level).
Queue and async processing is readiness-oriented, not yet full queue infrastructure.
Test/eval foundations exist, but complete end-to-end critical-path coverage is not fully obvious from repo alone.
Some institutional controls are visibility/foundation-level rather than full enterprise compliance workflows.
Internal billing IDs (free/pro/agency) differ from public packaging labels (Starter/Growth/Enterprise), increasing naming complexity.
Some in-app copy still contains broader persona references that can dilute strict agency-only positioning.

maturity_assessment.reasoning: Core paid workflow is implemented end-to-end with real integrations and multitenancy/security foundations, while several scale and parity areas remain intentionally partial.
